opts: A data structure that follows LIFO principle
(B) A data structure that follows FIFO principle
(C) A data structure that follows random access
(D) A data structure that follows heap principle
questions.explanation: A stack is a linear data structure that follows the LIFO (Last In First Out) principle.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of push operation in stack?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of push operation in stack is O(1), as it involves just adding an element at the top.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of pop operation in stack?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of pop operation in stack is O(1), as it involves just removing an element from the top.

questions.q: What is the use of a stack in compilers?
questions.explanation: A stack is used to store the intermediate results during the compilation process.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of searching an element in stack?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of searching an element in stack is O(1), as it involves just checking the top element.

questions.q: What is the use of a stack in expression evaluation?
opts: (D) To evaluate the postfix expressions and infix expressions
questions.explanation: A stack is used to evaluate the postfix and infix expressions.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of reversing a linked list using stack?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of reversing a linked list using stack is O(n), as it involves pushing each node onto the stack and then popping each node off the stack.

questions.q: What is the space complexity of reversing a linked list using stack?
questions.explanation: The space complexity of reversing a linked list using stack is O(n), as it involves storing each node in the stack.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of solving the Tower of Hanoi problem using stack?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of solving the Tower of Hanoi problem using stack is O(2^n), as it involves recursively moving the disks.

questions.q: What is the space complexity of solving the Tower of Hanoi problem using stack?
questions.explanation: The space complexity of solving the Tower of Hanoi problem using stack is O(2^n), as it involves storing each move in the stack.

questions.q: What is the use of a stack in parsing the syntax of a programming language?
opts: To parse the syntax of the language
(B) To generate the syntax of the language
questions.explanation: A stack is used to store the syntax trees during the parsing process.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of evaluating a postfix expression using stack?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of evaluating a postfix expression using stack is O(n), as it involves popping each operator off the stack.

questions.q: What is the space complexity of evaluating a postfix expression using stack?
questions.explanation: The space complexity of evaluating a postfix expression using stack is O(n), as it involves storing each operand in the stack.

questions.q: What is the use of a stack in implementing recursive algorithms?
questions.explanation: A stack is used to store the recursive calls during the execution of recursive algorithms.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of implementing the recursive algorithms using stack?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of implementing the recursive algorithms using stack is O(n), as it involves pushing each recursive call onto the stack.

questions.q: What is the space complexity of implementing the recursive algorithms using stack?
questions.explanation: The space complexity of implementing the recursive algorithms using stack is O(n), as it involves storing each recursive call in the stack.

questions.q: What is the use of a stack in implementing the undo and redo functions?
opts: To implement the undo and redo functions
(B) To implement the copy and paste functions
(C) To store the undo and redo history
questions.explanation: A stack is used to store the undo and redo history during the execution of the undo and redo functions.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of implementing the undo and redo functions using stack?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of implementing the undo and redo functions using stack is O(n), as it involves pushing each undo and redo command onto the stack.

questions.q: What is the space complexity of implementing the undo and redo functions using stack?
questions.explanation: The space complexity of implementing the undo and redo functions using stack is O(n), as it involves storing each undo and redo command in the stack.
